title: Neck: Fascial layers and tissue spaces
body_html:
The neck has multiple layers of fascia. It can be simply divided into superficial and deep fascia. The deep fascia can be further subdivided into four well defined layers.

Superficial fascia
- Thin layer that encloses platysma and contains superficial neurovascular supply including cutaneonus nerves

Deep superficila facsia
  • Investing fascia
  • Prevertebral fascia
  • Pretracheal fascia
  • Carotid sheath

Image illustrating the fascial layers of the neck
